name: SQUIRE Barbershop Management API
what_it_does: SQUIRE REST API for barbershop booking and management platform. Enables AI agents to manage appointment booking and barber scheduling, handle client profiles and haircut preference history, process point-of-sale transactions and service payments, manage shop membership and prepaid packages, access waitlist and walk-in queue management, retrieve business analytics and barber performance data, handle product retail sales, and integrate barbershop data with marketing and customer engagement platforms.
use_cases: Managing appointment booking and barber scheduling from shop operations agents
Processing payments and memberships from billing automation agents
Accessing client preference history from personalization agents
Retrieving barber performance analytics from business intelligence agents
not_for: Full-service salons without barbershop-specific queue and walk-in management
Hair salons requiring appointment-only without walk-in queue workflows
Medical aesthetic services without basic barbershop focus
best_when: A barbershop or barber chain using SQUIRE wants AI agents to automate booking, walk-in queue management, membership billing, and client engagement workflows.
avoid_when: PRIVACY: Client service history and personal data require careful handling. Payment data requires PCI-DSS compliance. Automated marketing outreach must comply with CAN-SPAM and TCPA.

auth.notes: SQUIRE uses API key and OAuth 2.0 for API access. Shop-level credentials. Partner program for integrations. Webhooks for real-time booking and payment events. Developer documentation available for partners.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — barbershop subscription pricing
pricing.paid_tiers: Monthly per-shop pricing; enterprise pricing for barbershop chains
pricing.notes: New York City. Founded 2015. Dominant barbershop management platform. Raised $165M Series C (2021). 4,000+ shops. Focus on urban barbershops with walk-in and appointment hybrid model. Competes with Booksy for barbershop market. Strong demographic: predominantly African American barbershop owners.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API client; documented in developer portal

known_agent_gotchas: PRIVACY: Client service history and payment data requires care
No public MCP server — OAuth2 REST API with partner program
Walk-in queue management is a core differentiator — automation must handle real-time queue dynamics
Barbershop-specific scheduling rules — booking buffers and no-show policies differ from salons
Marketing outreach must comply with TCPA for text-based client notifications
Tipping flows through POS — tip handling automation requires careful payment split logic
agent_readiness.security_notes: Client PII and payment data. PCI-DSS, SOC2. OAuth2 + API key. US data residency. TCPA for text marketing.

security_score_components.security_notes: Client PII and payment data. PCI-DSS, SOC2. OAuth2. US data residency. TCPA compliance for marketing.
